action.reasoning: Observation: bitsandbytes cannot be installed; QLoRA impossible. Full-precision LoRA OOM'd earlier due to memory spikes. However, with device_map='auto' on 2x32GB GPUs, gradient checkpointing, reduced max_pixels to limit visual tokens, and careful loss computation in bfloat16, we can fit training. We need to test with a small subset (500 samples, 1 epoch) to confirm no OOM, measure iteration time, and obtain a baseline grounding_score (expect >0 due to improved training with proper format). Analysis: previous 500-sample model had near-zero IoU but that may be due to data insufficiency and scaling errors. With proper prompting and consistent parser, even 500 samples might give some non-zero score. Decision: write a robust training script with LoRA (rank 16), full dataset option but test with 500 samples, 1 epoch, val IoU monitoring, merge best model, and submit eval immediately after training. This will validate the training pipeline and obtain a baseline without risk of OOM. If successful and grounding_score >0, we can scale up in next iterations.
